item.title: CVE-2025-10035
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5
Based on writing the Rapid7 Analysis, I have rated the Attacker Value as Very High, a file transfer solution is a very high value target for an attacker, and this vulnerability is unauthenticated RCE. However, based on our analysis, the attacker will require knowledge or a specific private key to correctly sign a payload in order to exploit successfully, I have added the Difficult to weaponize tag. For an attacker who can satisfy the private key requirement, exploitability would be Very High, but for all other attackers the exploitability is Very Low.

item.title: CVE-2025-20363
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 3/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2025-20363 is a heap-based buffer overflow affecting many popular Cisco products: Cisco ASA, FTD, IOS, IOS XE, and IOS XR. When Cisco ASA or FTD are exploited, credentials are not required; when Cisco IOS, IOS XE, or IOS XR are exploited, low-privilege credentials are required. In all scenarios, the result is root-level RCE on the affected system. The CVE entry for CVE-2025-20363 lists over 2,500 versions affected.
Cisco is a huge target for threat actors, as their products are popular. In particular, unauthenticated root-level remote code execution on Cisco ASA is a bit of a holy grail…

item.title: CVE-2025-20333
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
This is an authentication bypass affecting Cisco Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) and Cisco Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) appliances. It is known to be exploited in -the-wild, and has been chained with an separate RCE vulnerability, CVE-2025-20333 (according to CISA), to allow a remote unauthenticated attacker achieve unauthenticated RCE against a target device.
According to the vendor advisory, a device is not vulnerable in a default configuration, but rather vulnerable in one of several configuration. However these configuration are likely very common in an enterprise setting, e.g. an…

item.title: CVE-2025-20362
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
This is a memory corruption vulnerability (the CWE indicates it is a buffer overflow) affecting Cisco Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) and Cisco Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) appliances. It is known to be exploited in-the-wild, and has been chained with an separate authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2025-20362 (according to CISA), to allow a remote unauthenticated attacker achieve unauthenticated RCE with root privileges against a target device.
According to the vendor advisory, an attacker requires "valid VPN user credentials" in order to exploit, however CISA has indicated in the…

item.title: Assessment 5e35a669
item.description:
Attacker Value: 3/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
On June 30, 2025, a privilege escalation vulnerability in the popular sudo command was published. The vulnerability exploited the chroot function of sudo, which was introduced in June 2023, in version 1.9.14.
Improved command matching when a chroot is specified in sudoers. The sudoers plugin will now change the root directory id needed before performing command matching. Previously, the root directory was simply prepared to the path that was being processed.
The consequence of this functionality is that an unprivileged user can invoke chroot() on a writable location. As describe…

item.title: CVE-2025-41244
item.description:
Attacker Value: 3/5 | Exploitability: 2/5
A local privilege escalation in VMware guest service discovery (open-vm-tools / VMware Aria Operations SDMP). A logic flaw in version-discovery handling uses overly-broad regexes and allows execution of attacker-controlled binaries from user-writable paths (e.g., /tmp), enabling an unprivileged guest user to get code executed in a privileged context.
Exploitation is only possible if the target VM is managed by VMware Aria Operations and has the proprietary SDMP service enabled. This prevents arbitrary exploitation across all systems merely running VMware Tools. The presence of the Aria Ope…

item.title: CVE-2025-61882
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
The exploit for this was leaked, and has been available on VirusTotal since 3 Oct 2025 (both exp.py and server.py). A port of the leaked exploit is also on GitHub.
As there is publicly available exploit code, exploitability for the vuln is now Very High. We know that a ransomware group has used this exploit already as a zero day to exploit victims in a ransomware campaign, so the attacker value here is also Very High. The exploit itself achieves unauthenticated RCE as the oracle user (so not root), but this should not be a mitigating factor in its impact. Broad exploitation is now likely to…

item.title: Assessment 85091c37
item.description: Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Execution

item.title: Assessment eb9d84e4
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 3/5
Kaspersky Labs discovered this 0-day in Chrome Browser and reported it to Google. The 0-day was abused as part of a targeted campaign, as described in their blog: https://securelist.com/forumtroll-apt-hacking-team-dante-spyware/117851/

item.title: Assessment a51ed820
item.description: Attacker Value: 3/5 | Exploitability: 4/5

item.title: CVE-2025-5946
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 4/5 | MITRE: Execution
Centreon is a platform designed to monitor your cloud and on-premises infrastructure. The platform has command injection vulnerability using the broker engine reload setting on the poller configuration page of the Centreon web application. Injecting a malcious payload at the broker engine reload parameter and restarting the poller, triggers this vulnerability. You need have admin access at the Centreon Web application in order to execute this RCE.
Demonstration 1. Download and install Centreon web application using one of supported install methods. 2. When installed, login into Centreo…

item.title: CVE-2025-12480
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
Based on writing the Rapid7 Analysis, I have rated both the Exploitability and Attacker Value as Very High, as the target product is a file sharing solution, and is vulnerable to unauthenticated remote code execution in a default configuration.

item.title: CVE-2025-64446
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
FortiWeb is a Web Application Firewall (WAF) product that is designed to detect and block malicious traffic to web applications. Exploitation of this vulnerability, now tracked as CVE-2025-64446, allows an attacker with no existing level of access to gain administrator-level access to the FortiWeb Manager panel and websocket command-line interface. Unsurprisingly, this is a critical severity vulnerability, with a CVSS score of 9.1.
The vulnerability details were first shared publicly on October 6, 2025, when the cyber deception company Defused published a proof-of-concept exploit on social …

item.title: CVE-2025-64446
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Execution
8.0.1
CVE-2025-64446 – Fortinet FortiWeb Auth. Bypass | Path Traversal Vulnerability What is: It is a full authentication‑bypass exploit that creates a new admin user on a FortiWeb device — including:
- Path traversal - Forged CGIINFO header - Admin‐privilege creation - API abuse to push a new local user
Overview CVE-2025-64446 is a critical path traversal vulnerability affecting multiple versions of Fortinet FortiWeb, a Web Application Firewall (WAF) used to protect web applications and APIs. The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to send specially c…

item.title: Assessment ccb5097e
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
In one of our honeypots we observed the following payload being executed:
GET /api/fabric/device/status HTTP/1.1 User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.0; ber-MA; rv:1.9.0.20) Gecko/2976-07-21 17:07:38 Firefox/6.0 Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate Accept: / Connection: keep-alive Authorization: Bearer ';select//a//from//fabricuser.a//into//outfile//'/var/log/lib/python3.10/pylab.py'//FIELDS//ESCAPED//BY//'

item.title: Assessment fbacb2a6
item.description:
Attacker Value: 2/5 | Exploitability: 1/5
Note: The following analysis was written by the original finder, Calum Hutton.
Details A malicious client acting as the receiver of an rsync file transfer can trigger an OOB read via a negative array index. The rsync client requires at least read access to the remote rsync module to trigger the issue.
The protocol traffic for the following rsync command was captured:
rsync -vvr rsync://192.168.178.93/filesanon /tmp/rsync
Part of the initial protocol traffic sends the following data from the client (spaces represent null bytes):
--server --sender -vvre.iLsfxCIvu . filesanon/ …

item.title: CVE-2025-58034
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
Based on writing the Rapid7 Analysis, I have rated the exploitability as Very High, as exploitation is trivial and reliable. The Attacker Value is High as you do need an auth bypass to chain to (or other valid admin credentials).
While this is authenticated, the auth bypass vulnerability CVE-2025-64446 can be used to create a new local administrator account, making chaining CVE-2025-64446 + CVE-2025-58034 equivalent to unauthenticated RCE.

item.title: CVE-2025-64446
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Execution
CVE-2025-64446 — LAB-SAFE Proof of Concept Author: nu11secur1ty Status: LAB-SAFE / NON-EXPLOITABLE Purpose: Research, Education & Vendor Reporting Only
---
🔒 IMPORTANT NOTICE This README accompanies a LAB‑SAFE, non‑harmful, sanitized PoC designed exclusively for: - Security research - Vendor coordination - Documentation - Demonstration of non-sensitive behavior
All dangerous exploitation steps, privilege‑escalation mechanics, auth‑bypass logic, and sensitive payloads are removed or neutralized to comply with safety and legal guidelines.
---
📌 CVE Desc…

item.title: CVE-2020-2555
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Execution
Attack Vector - Protocol: T3 - Authentication: Not Required - Complexity: Low - Impact: Full System Compromise
Impact
- Remote code execution with WebLogic server privileges - Complete system access - Potential domain compromise - Data exfiltration capabilities
Detection
Organizations can detect exploitation attempts by monitoring for: - Unusual T3 protocol traffic patterns - Suspicious deserialization events in WebLogic logs - Unexpected process execution from WebLogic JVM
Mitigation
Immediate Actions 1. Apply latest Oracle Critical Patch Updates 2. Restrict …

item.title: CVE-2025-59287
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 4/5
In October 2025, Microsoft released a patch for a deserialization vulnerability in Windows Server Update Service (WSUS). WSUS is a centralized Microsoft update distribution service for administrators. The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to send a request with a deserialized malicious payload, which can reach the vulnerable soapFormatter. The vulnerability can be exploited by a series of requests to WSUS, where the malicious payload is contained in the AuthorizationCookie vulnerability. The vulnerable soapFormatter can be reached by sending a request to /ReportingWebService/Rep…

item.title: CVE-2025-55182
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
Unauthenticated RCE which affects React applications that support React Server Components, and affects multiple downstream frameworks such as Next.js. A target is exploitable in a default configuration if the application supports React Server Components, and even if the application does not explicitly implement any React Server Function endpoints. The vulnerability was originally discovered by Lachlan Davidson, and has been dubbed React2Shell by the finder.
While multiple non-viable PoC's were posted shortly after public disclosure on December 3, a validated RCE PoC was posted by maple3142 …

item.title: Assessment 33512c9d
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 2/5
Pros:
- Gains RCE
Cons:
- Requires minimagick set as image processor in Active Storage configuration - Requires the application send untrusted user input in transformation method or its arguments

item.title: CVE-2025-59718
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2025-59718 and CVE-2025-59719 are Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature vulnerabilities affecting several popular Fortinet products. The affected systems are vulnerable in a non-default configuration. However, Fortinet's advisory notes that the vulnerable configuration is enabled "when an administrator registers the device to FortiCare from the device's GUI", if the administrator doesn't specifically opt out. As such, the vulnerable configuration may be default, depending on how the device is set up.
Since the vulnerable configuration is ambiguous, and since exploitation in …

item.title: CVE-2025-59718
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2025-59718 and CVE-2025-59719 are Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature vulnerabilities affecting several popular Fortinet products. The affected systems are vulnerable in a non-default configuration. However, Fortinet's advisory notes that the vulnerable configuration is enabled "when an administrator registers the device to FortiCare from the device's GUI", if the administrator doesn't specifically opt out. As such, the vulnerable configuration may be default, depending on how the device is set up.
Since the vulnerable configuration is ambiguous, and since exploitation of …

item.title: CVE-2025-37164
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 4/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
On December 16, 2025, Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) published an advisory for CVE-2025-37164, a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability affecting HPE OneView. The vulnerability, which was reported to HPE by security researcher Nguyen Quoc Khanh, facilitates unauthenticated remote code execution on versions of HPE OneView before 11.0. For more information about the product and advisory, read the Rapid7 Blog.
Exploitation against vulnerable instances is straightforward, requiring only a single unauthenticated web request to run arbitrary commands. I've rated 'Attacker Value' as 'High', since HPE OneView…

item.title: CVE-2025-14847
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5
CVE-2025-14847 is a remote memory disclosure vulnerability in MongoDB. It allows an unauthenticated attacker to leak the server's heap memory by exploiting the interaction between the OPCOMPRESSED wire protocol message and the BSON parser. The mechanism is functionally similar to "Heartbleed" but specific to MongoDB's handling of compressed data buffers.
The vulnerability relies on the server trusting the client's declared uncompressedsize without proper initialization of the allocated memory.
Based on the analysis of a few public POCs: The attacker sends a compressed message (using zli…

item.title: Assessment 5c94319d
item.description: Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 4/5

item.title: CVE-2026-21858
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 2/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
On November 18, 2025, a patched release was published for a critical unauthenticated file read vulnerability in n8n, a popular piece of automation software. The advisory for this vulnerability, CVE-2026-21858, was subsequently published on January 7, 2026; the vulnerability holds a CVSS score of 10.0. If a server has a custom configured web form that implements file uploads with no validation of content type, an attacker can overwrite an internal JSON object to read arbitrary files and gain administrator access to n8n. In some cases, exploitation will result in remote code execution on the und…

item.title: CVE-2025-22224
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 3/5 | MITRE: Privilege Escalation
CVE-2025-22224 is an out-of-bounds write in Broadcom VMware ESXi and Workstation, a desirable type of bug attackers use to escape from a virtualized execution context and take over the underlying VMware cluster host. The official advisory for the vulnerability was first published on March 4, 2025, along with advisories for an information leak vulnerability and a sandbox escape vulnerability; all three vulnerabilities were designated to have been observed being leveraged in the wild as zero-day exploits.
On January 7, 2026, the security firm Huntress published an excellent technical analysis…

item.title: CVE-2025-22225
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 3/5 | MITRE: Privilege Escalation
CVE-2025-22225 is an arbitrary write vulnerability in Broadcom VMware ESXi and Workstation that can be used for sandbox escape. This bug is a desirable type of vulnerability that attackers can exploit in a chain to assist with escaping from a virtualized execution context and taking over the underlying VMware cluster host. The official advisory for the vulnerability was first published on March 4, 2025, along with advisories for an ASLR information leak vulnerability and a TOCTOU out-of-bounds write vulnerability; all three vulnerabilities were designated to have been observed being leveraged …

item.title: CVE-2025-22226
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Privilege Escalation
CVE-2025-22226 is an information leak in Broadcom VMware ESXi and Workstation that can be used to defeat the ASLR exploit mitigation. This bug is a desirable type of vulnerability that attackers can exploit in a chain to assist with escaping from a virtualized execution context and taking over the underlying VMware cluster host. The official advisory for the vulnerability was first published on March 4, 2025, along with advisories for a TOCTOU out-of-bounds write vulnerability and a sandbox escape write vulnerability; all three vulnerabilities were designated to have been observed being levera…

item.title: CVE-2024-52547
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 4/5
The vulnerability represents a weakness in the device's kernel-level code signing enforcement mechanism. Despite having a CVSS score of 6.7 (Medium) due to requiring local high-privilege access, when combined with CVE-2024-52547's command execution capability, it enables complete arbitrary code execution.
The Lorex camera implements kernel-level code signing enforcement using files named SigFilePartition, SigFileList, and DataSignature distributed across the filesystem. These files contain lists of protected paths and corresponding cryptographic signatures, preventing the execution of unsi…

item.title: CVE-2024-52548
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 3/5
The vulnerability exists in the DHIP service over TCP port 80 and affects the configManager.getConfig command handler. With a CVSS score of 7.2 (High), it reflects the significant impact achievable by an authenticated attacker. Lorex remediated this vulnerability in their November 25, 2024 firmware update.
The vulnerability occurs in a filter function processing configManager.getConfig requests. When processing the "name" parameter, the function searches for a period character and calculates the string length up to that character. This length is used in a strncpy call to copy the name into …

item.title: CVE-2024-52544
item.description:
Attacker Value: 2/5 | Exploitability: 3/5
The vulnerability exists in the MulticastaccessInit function, exposed via the DevInit.access command on the DHIP service. This function expects a JSON object containing a "name" string and a "pwd" string. While the code validates that the "name" item is a string type (checking nodetype & 0x10), it fails to perform the same validation on the "pwd" item. The code then passes pwditem-valuestr directly to strncpy. If an attacker provides a non-string type for "pwd" (such as a number), the JSON parsing library stores the value in a different member variable, leaving valuestr as null. This results i…

item.title: CVE-2024-52544
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 3/5
The flaw exists in the IQ (Image Quality) service, which normally does not run by default but can be activated by exploiting CVE-2024-52544. The vulnerability received a CVSS score of 6.5 (Medium), acknowledging that while exploitation requires specific conditions, successful exploitation leads to significant information disclosure.
The IQ Service uses a custom binary protocol on TCP port 9876. The function MIIQSERVERGetApi handles command ID 6 and reads a 16-bit maxword value from attacker-controlled input data. This value directly controls the output buffer's currlength field via the calc…

item.title: CVE-2024-52545
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 4/5
The vulnerability resides in the DP Service listening on TCP port 3500. The login handler function processes authentication requests containing username and password fields delimited by double ampersands ("&&"). Neither field is length-validated before being copied via memcpy into fixed 128-byte stack buffers. An attacker can supply an oversized username or password value to overflow the stack buffer, overwriting the saved return address. Although the non-PIE binary allows predictable code addresses, all addresses contain null bytes (0x00XXXXXX), and full ASLR is enabled. The exploit overcomes…

item.title: CVE-2024-6235
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 3/5
This vulnerability was discovered by Rapid7, during root cause analysis of a separate vulnerability (CVE-2024-6235) in Citrix NetScaler Console. NetScaler Console is an enterprise policy management platform frequently deployed at network edges, making it an attractive target for ransomware operators and advanced threat actors. The vulnerability affects NetScaler Console and NetScaler SDX versions 14.1 before 14.1.47.46 and 13.1 before 13.1.58.32.
The vulnerability exists in the NetScaler Console download API at the /nitro/v1/download endpoint. The API supports an alternative usage pattern w…

item.title: CVE-2024-12284
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 3/5
This vulnerability was also discovered by Rapid7 during analysis of the NetScaler Console platform. Initially, Citrix indicated the issue was already fixed in their latest version and declined to assign a CVE. However, after further investigation in June 2025, the vendor determined the vulnerability is identified as CVE-2024-12284. It affects NetScaler Console and NetScaler Agent versions 14.1 before 14.1-38.53 and 13.1 before 13.1-56.18.
The vulnerability is a classic ZipSlip attack targeting the StyleBook import functionality. The StyleBook API, a Python microservice, allows administrator…

item.title: CVE-2024-55591
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2024-55591 is a critical severity authentication bypass affecting FortiOS and FortiProxy, facilitating full administrator-level control of the device with no existing level of access. The vulnerability's official advisory was published on January 14, 2025. At the time of publication, the vulnerability was being exploited in the wild, per a zero-day exploitation report by Arctic Wolf. Subsequently, security firm watchTowr published an excellent technical analysis of the vulnerability, which outlines the details of exploitation.
The vulnerability is a complex one, encompassing a WebSocket…

item.title: Assessment 4005a3f2
item.description:
MITRE: Initial Access
This vulnerability allows scripting commands to load from SharePoint servers and one drive, when signing into OneDrive the malicious software will load onto the computer during sync operations and land as a vault executable. This vulnerability allows modifying of files and photos and is the attack entry point for creating a shadow account which then allows authorization for multiple sign ins and unauthorized access to bookmarks, history and credentials. They create scripts and save their commands and scripts inside of SharePoint and use graph connections to control AI services, which they can…

item.title: Assessment 5c791ddf
item.description:
Attacker Value: 2/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
Summary
Out of the box, MCPJam runs without auth disabled and on 0.0.0.0, and local auth is not available; it has to be configured to auth to MCPJams servers (https://login.mcpjam.com/)
The patch only changes the default listener to run only on localhost, not on 0.0.0.0, meaning that if the user chooses to make it available on all interfaces, it will still be vulnerable.
If adding optional Auth in testing on the latest version the RCE is still achievable without needing auth. Auth appears to be for Cloud Sync of Secrets only not protecting access to the tool or the endpoints.
R…

item.title: CVE-2026-1731
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
On February 6, 2026, BeyondTrust released security advisory BT26-02, disclosing a critical remote code execution vulnerability affecting its Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products. Reported by Hacktron AI researchers and assigned CVE-2026-1731, exploitation allows unauthenticated, remote attackers to execute arbitrary operating system commands in the context of the site user by sending specially crafted requests. The vulnerability affects Remote Support (RS) versions 25.3.1 and prior, as well as Privileged Remote Access (PRA) versions 24.3.4 and prior.
While Beyond…

item.title: CVE-2026-1281
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340 are two very similar critical vulnerabilities affecting Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) that were announced on January 29, 2026. The impact of exploitation of the vulnerabilities is unauthenticated root-level remote code execution on a sensitive mobile device endpoint management system, making these bugs valuable to attackers; an attacker that compromises EPMM would typically gain access to a wealth of PII collected from joined mobile endpoints, along with a privileged position over the devices themselves. Notably, at the time of disclosure, Ivanti advised…

item.title: CVE-2026-1340
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2026-1340 and CVE-2026-1281 are two very similar critical vulnerabilities affecting Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) that were announced on January 29, 2026. The impact of exploitation of the vulnerabilities is unauthenticated root-level remote code execution on a sensitive mobile device endpoint management system, making these bugs valuable to attackers; an attacker that compromises EPMM would typically gain access to a wealth of PII collected from joined mobile endpoints, along with a privileged position over the devices themselves. Notably, at the time of disclosure, Ivanti advised…

item.title: CVE-2025-40551
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2025-40551 is one of four critical vulnerabilities affecting SolarWinds Web Help Desk that were patched on January 28, 2026. The vulnerability, which was reported to SolarWinds by Jimi Sebree of Horizon3.ai, is an unsafe deserialization bug, and it facilitates unauthenticated remote code execution on affected Web Help Desk systems. The issue is a patch bypass for a vulnerability that had two prior patch bypasses - CVE-2024-28986, a deserialization security flaw in the AjaxProxy component. This vulnerability, along with patch bypasses, have been added to the CISA KEV catalog.
CVE-2025-4…

item.title: CVE-2026-20127
item.description:
Attacker Value: 5/5 | Exploitability: 3/5 | MITRE: Impact
CVE-2026-20127 is a critical authentication bypass vulnerability affecting Cisco Catalyst SD‑WAN Controller and Cisco Catalyst SD‑WAN Manager that was patched on February 25, 2026. The vulnerability was originally identified and reported to Cisco by Australian Signals Directorate's Australian Cyber Security Centre as being exploited in the wild. This vulnerability, has been added to the CISA KEV catalog
CVE-2026-20127 is an authentication bypass vulnerability affecting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager systems. Exploitation allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to submit spec…

item.title: CVE-2026-28209
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 4/5 | MITRE: Execution
A command injection vulnerability exists in FreePBX when using the ElevenLabs Text-to-Speech (TTS) engine. User-controlled input is passed unsanitized to a shell command executed via exec(), allowing authenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on the FreePBX server. The vulnerability can be triggered via an AJAX endpoint in the System Recordings module and affects users with access to the recordings functionality.
Affected Product Product: FreePBX Module: System Recordings Driver: ElevenLabs TTS File: admin/modules/recordings/drivers/Elevenlabs.php Function: …

item.title: CVE-2026-28287
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 5/5 | MITRE: Execution
A command injection vulnerability exists in FreePBX. User-controlled input is passed unsanitized to a shell command executed via exec(), allowing authenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on the FreePBX server. The vulnerability can be triggered via an AJAX endpoint in the System Recordings module and affects users with access to the recordings functionality.
Affected Products Product: FreePBX Module: System Recordings File: admin/modules/recordings/Recordings.class.php Function: function fixeRIFF($filename)
Tested Versions FreePBX 17.0.24 FreePBX 17.0…

item.title: CVE-2026-3055
item.description:
Attacker Value: 4/5 | Exploitability: 3/5 | MITRE: Initial Access
On March 23, 2026, Citrix published a security advisory for a critical vulnerability affecting their NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) products. This vulnerability, CVE-2026-3055, which is classified as an out-of-bounds read and holds a CVSS score of 9.3, allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak potentially sensitive information from the appliance's memory. The Citrix advisory states that systems configured as a SAML Identity Provider (SAML IDP) are vulnerable, whereas default configurations are unaffected.
As of the advisory’s public…

item.title: CVE-2026-3055
item.description:
MITRE: Initial Access
CVE-2026-3055 — Detection & Recon Analysis ---
1. Executive summary
| Aspect | Assessment | |--------|------------| | Purpose | Identify NetScaler ADC/Gateway surfaces that expose SAML/WS-Federation–related HTTP endpoints and return content consistent with Citrix IdP/federation behavior. | | CVE linkage | Template metadata maps to CVE-2026-3055, CWE-125, Citrix bulletin CTX696300, and a CVSS 4.0 score of 9.3 per embedded classification. | | What is proven | Exposure and configuration class (reachable endpoints + response shape). | | What is not proven | Exploitability, memory disc…
